name: Aiven Managed Data Infrastructure API
what_it_does: Multi-cloud managed data infrastructure platform with REST API for provisioning and managing PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka, OpenSearch, ClickHouse, and Flink services across AWS, GCP, Azure, and other clouds.
use_cases: Provisioning managed PostgreSQL or Redis instances programmatically via API
Scaling managed Kafka clusters based on throughput metrics from automation agents
Managing multi-cloud data service deployment and configuration as code
Querying service health, metrics, and logs for managed data services
Automating database backups, fork operations, and disaster recovery workflows
not_for: Self-hosted database management without cloud cost considerations
Single-database deployments where simpler managed services (RDS, ElastiCache) suffice
Organizations already deeply committed to a single cloud vendor's native data services
best_when: An agent manages multi-cloud data infrastructure — provisioning, scaling, and monitoring managed databases and streaming services across cloud providers.
avoid_when: You're single-cloud and happy with native managed services (AWS RDS, GCP Cloud SQL) — Aiven adds cost over native services.

auth.notes: Authentication tokens from Aiven Console with configurable scopes. Project-level and organization-level tokens. Service account tokens for automation. OpenAPI spec published for all endpoints.

pricing.free_tier_limits: $300 free credit for new accounts; free trial period
pricing.paid_tiers: PostgreSQL from ~$20/month (startup plan); Kafka from ~$100/month; pricing varies by cloud and region
pricing.notes: Usage-based pricing per service and plan tier. Prices vary by cloud provider, region, and service type. Transparent pricing calculator available. Enterprise discounts available.

performance.rate_limits: API: 10 create/delete operations per second; reads are higher

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAPI spec published and comprehensive. REST API well-documented for all service types. Error responses are JSON with detailed messages and codes. Service creation is async — polling for state transitions required.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Service creation with same name in same project returns existing service. Configuration updates are idempotent by design.
known_agent_gotchas: Service provisioning is async — poll service_state until RUNNING before use
Service name must be unique per project — check before creating to avoid conflicts
Different service types have different configuration schemas — validate per service type
Cross-cloud and cross-region service links have specific network requirements

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II, ISO 27001, GDPR, HIPAA, and PCI-DSS compliant. TLS for all data services enforced. Project-scoped authentication tokens. Regular security audits. EU-based company with strong data protection practices.
